text.title: backup-database-postgres Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the PostgreSQL backup pipeline. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Every pg_dump or pg_basebackup script MUST verify the produced artifact (`pg_restore -l` for custom format, `gunzip -t` for gzipped streams, `pg_verifybackup` for tar/plain base backups) BEFORE pushing to remote storage. A failed verification MUST trigger an alert and MUST NOT upload.

rationale.source: PostgreSQL docs — pg_verifybackup, internal restore-drill audits

rule.rationale: pg_dump can produce a partial file when the source crashes mid-export or disk fills; silent uploads create the illusion of a backup without the substance. Verification adds under one second for most database sizes and catches corruption at the producer.

rule.statement: Clusters using WAL archiving for PITR MUST monitor `pg_stat_archiver.failed_count` and alert when it increases, AND MUST set `archive_command` to a script that exits non-zero on remote write failure (no silent retries that swallow the exit code).
rule.rationale: archive_command failures are transient (network blip, S3 throttle) and often recover silently in production without log noise. Without explicit monitoring, the failure is discovered only during a restore attempt — the worst possible moment.

rule.statement: Production pg_dump scripts MUST use custom format (`-Fc`) or directory format (`-Fd`); plain SQL (`-Fp` or default) is forbidden for production backups because it cannot drive `pg_restore -j` parallel restore or selective table restore.
rule.rationale: Plain SQL format is human-readable but cannot be partially restored or parallel-restored. Directory format is required for `-j` parallelism; custom format is required for `pg_restore -L` filtering.

rule.statement: Backup scripts MUST load `PGPASSWORD`, `RESTIC_PASSWORD`, and S3 access keys from a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, GCP Secret Manager) or `~/.pgpass` with mode `0600`; never inline-literal in script source or commit metadata. A pre-commit grep MUST reject `RESTIC_PASSWORD=|PGPASSWORD=` in staged hunks.

rationale.source: OWASP Secrets Management, internal incident review

rule.rationale: Credential leakage in Git is a security incident with broad blast radius (replication, archive deletion). Encryption key loss = data loss; both modes are eliminated by mandatory secrets-manager pull.

rule.statement: If the cluster is fully managed PaaS (RDS, Cloud SQL, AlloyDB) WITH automated backups enabled AND there is no cross-account or cross-cloud copy requirement, this methodology MUST be skipped in favour of the platform's native backup; only enable when at least one of those conditions is false.

rationale.source: AWS RDS Backup docs, GCP Cloud SQL Backup docs

rule.rationale: Managed PaaS backup engines already implement integrity, retention, and PITR. Adding a parallel pipeline duplicates cost and introduces drift between two recovery paths.
